sections.paragraph:
ἀποθησαυρίζω (s. θησαυρίζω; Diod S 3, 31, 3; 5, 75, 4; Epict 3, 22, 50; Lucian, Alex. 23; Aelian, NA 14, 18; Artem. 1, 73 p. 66, 22; Vett. Val. 16, 21; 18:12; Jos., Bell. 7, 299 et al. [Nägeli 30]; Sir 3:4) store up, lay up fig. ἀ. θεμέλιον καλόν lay up a good foundation 1 Ti 6:19 (cp. θεμέλιος γὰρ τῷ φαύλῳ κακία καὶ πάθος=baseness and passion are a bad man’s support Philo, Sacr. Ab/C 81). The metaphor is “telescoped” (cp. Pind., P. 10, 51–54; s. BGildersleeve, Pindar 1885, 355).—DELG s.v. θησαυρός.

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

et et al. = et alii (and others)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 110
